guide.title: Health + Digestive Wellness Guide
guide.text: Discover how your body processes food — and how to work with it, not against it. Your report reveals the patterns behind your digestion, bloating, energy, cravings, and how your gut responds to different foods.

intro.title: Your Tongue Reveals Your Gut Health
intro.subtitle: In Ayurvedic and Traditional Chinese Medicine, the tongue is a mirror of your digestive system. Our AI reads colour, coating and texture — in seconds.

how.title: How to take the perfect scan
how.step1Desc: Stand near a window or turn on a light. Natural light gives the most accurate colour reading.
how.step2Desc: Relax and extend your tongue as far as comfortable. Make sure the whole surface is visible.
how.step3Desc: Best done in the morning before breakfast for the clearest reading.
how.step4Desc: Keep the camera 20–30 cm away. No zoom needed — a clear, well-lit close-up is enough.

camera.deniedSub: Please allow camera access in your browser settings, or use the gallery option below.

results.title: Scan Results
results.insightLabel: What this means for your gut
results.disclaimer: This scan is for informational purposes only and is not a medical diagnosis.

sticky.info: No account needed · 100% private · Photo stays on device

p1.title: Digestive Imbalance Detected

color.desc: Suggests liver heat and mild Pitta imbalance. Common in people who eat processed or spicy foods regularly.

coating.desc: Indicates Ama (undigested toxins) in the gut. Often linked to sluggish digestion and irregular meal times.

hydration.desc: Tongue surface shows early signs of dryness. You may not be drinking enough water between meals.

texture.desc: Tooth marks along the edges suggest dampness and spleen deficiency — a classic sign of digestive stress.

p1.insight: Your tongue shows classic signs of digestive imbalance — white–yellow coating and pale colour point to accumulated toxins (Ama) and possible dairy or gluten sensitivity. Many people with this profile find they feel heavy after meals, experience afternoon energy dips, and may have irregular bowel movements.

r1.title: Try a 7-day dairy-free trial
r1.body: Your coating pattern is strongly associated with dairy sensitivity. 72% of users with this profile feel lighter after cutting dairy.

r2.title: Eat your largest meal at lunch
r2.body: Digestive fire (Agni) peaks at midday. Shifting calories earlier reduces the coating and bloating you may experience.

r3.title: Sip warm water throughout the day
r3.body: Cold water slows digestion. Warm or room-temperature water helps clear coating and supports enzyme activity.

r4.title: Add ginger before meals
r4.body: Fresh ginger stimulates digestive juices and helps break down Ama. Try ½ tsp grated ginger with lemon 10 min before eating.

p2.title: Significant Gut Stress Found

color.desc: Indicates blood stagnation and heat in the body. Often linked to high stress, poor circulation, or chronic inflammation.

coating.desc: Heavy coating concentrated at the back of the tongue — this zone corresponds to the large intestine and kidneys in TCM.

hydration.desc: Tongue is visibly dry with reduced moisture. This slows digestion and reduces nutrient absorption significantly.

texture.desc: Cracks in the tongue body suggest chronic dehydration, Yin deficiency, or prolonged stress on the digestive system.

p2.insight: Your scan reveals significant digestive stress — the purple-red colour combined with a thick yellow coating is a classic presentation of heat toxins and gut inflammation in both Ayurvedic and TCM frameworks. People with this profile often report frequent bloating, constipation or diarrhoea, skin breakouts, and low energy.

r1.title: Cool your diet for 2 weeks
r1.body: Reduce spicy, fried, and alcohol. Add cooling foods: cucumber, mint, coconut water, and leafy greens.

r2.title: Prioritise anti-inflammatory foods
r2.body: Blueberries, turmeric, fatty fish (or flaxseed), and dark leafy greens directly target the inflammation pattern shown in your scan.

r3.title: Protect your sleep
r3.body: Your tongue pattern is strongly associated with elevated cortisol. Aim for a consistent 10pm bedtime.

r4.title: Add 10 min of daily breathwork
r4.body: Box breathing (4-4-4-4) reduces gut inflammation markers by lowering cortisol.

p3.title: Mostly Balanced — Minor Signs

color.desc: Healthy base colour. Slightly pale edges may indicate mild Vata imbalance or occasional blood sugar fluctuations.

coating.desc: A thin white coat is completely normal and healthy. Your digestive system is processing food well with minimal Ama.

hydration.desc: Tongue surface shows good moisture. Your fluid intake appears adequate. Maintain current water consumption.

texture.desc: Minor scalloping at the tip may indicate occasional stress or irregular sleep. Generally a minor finding.

p3.insight: Your tongue is showing a mostly healthy digestive system — good news! The main signals are occasional stress patterns (scalloped tip) and a slight tendency towards Vata imbalance.

r1.title: Establish consistent meal times
r1.body: Your Vata pattern responds well to routine. Eating at the same time each day significantly reduces evening bloating.

r2.title: Watch your fibre balance
r2.body: Add a daily tablespoon of ground flaxseed to support your gut microbiome optimally.

r3.title: Add CCF tea after dinner
r3.body: Cumin, Coriander, and Fennel tea is a classic Ayurvedic digestive tonic — perfect for your profile.

r4.title: Track your gut for 7 days
r4.body: You're close to optimal. A 7-day food and symptom log will reveal the 1–2 specific foods causing the stress pattern.

p4.title: Lactose & Gluten Signals Detected

color.desc: Geographic tongue pattern with white patches may indicate food sensitivities — particularly dairy and gluten reactions in the gut lining.

coating.desc: Uneven coating distribution is characteristic of absorption issues and food sensitivity reactions.

hydration.desc: Hydration is acceptable but could be improved. Increased water intake will help flush the coating and support gut barrier repair.

texture.desc: Areas of smooth, shiny tongue surface indicate possible B12 or iron deficiency — nutrients most affected by gut malabsorption.

p4.insight: Your tongue is showing a classic sensitivity pattern — the patchy coating and colour variation are hallmarks of immune reactivity to specific foods, most commonly dairy, gluten, and sometimes eggs.

r1.title: Try a strict 14-day dairy elimination
r1.body: This is the single highest-impact change for your tongue pattern. Remove all dairy for 14 days and photograph your tongue each morning.

r2.title: Consider a 7-day gluten-free trial
r2.body: After dairy, gluten is the second most common trigger for this coating pattern.

r3.title: Add gut-lining support foods
r3.body: Bone broth, collagen peptides, L-glutamine (from plant sources), and colourful vegetables support tight junction repair.

r4.title: Check B12 and iron levels
r4.body: Your texture changes suggest possible malabsorption. Ask your GP for a simple blood panel.
